pack_name: Missouri — SATOP, Outpatient SUD, CSTAR, REACT
regulatory_authority: Missouri Department of Mental Health, Division of Behavioral Health
disclaimer: Deterministic rule definitions for engine enforcement. Thresholds and citations transcribed from source regulatory analysis; verify against current CSR text before production enforcement. This pack drives counts/verdicts; explanatory text for staff/clients is served separately via the RAG knowledge base and is never the source of a compliance verdict.

credentials._comment: Credential definitions used by SIGNATURE/CREDENTIAL rules. A staff profile must carry one of these verified codes to perform the gated action.

PROVISIONAL_LMHP.label: Provisional LMHP (PLPC / LMSW)
PROVISIONAL_LMHP.note: LMSW may render diagnoses only if under registered supervision for LCSW; unsupervised LMSW must be blocked from diagnostic execution.

QAP.label: Qualified Addiction Professional / QMHP
QAP.note: May gather clinical data and draft; diagnostic and clinical summary must be routed to and co-signed by an LMHP (dual-signature workflow).

SQP.label: SATOP Qualified Professional
SQP.note: OMU screenings executed independently; WIP individual sessions require clinical supervisor oversight.

QSAP.label: Qualified Substance Abuse Professional
QSAP.note: Required to deliver intensive outpatient treatment (CIP, YCIP, SROP). Physician, licensed QMHP w/ 1yr SUD experience, MCB-certified/registered, or associate within 1yr under approved training plan.

ASSOCIATE.label: Associate Counselor (RASAC I / RASAC II)
ASSOCIATE.note: May deliver individual/group counseling and group education only under direct oversight and full professional responsibility of a trained clinical supervisor.

rules.label: Informed treatment consent (annual)
rules.requirement: Written informed consent executed at intake and renewed at least every 365 days.

rules.label: Initial admission goal at intake
rules.requirement: At least one preliminary treatment target documented at the time of intake before client can move to active status.

rules.label: Treatment plan within first 3 outpatient visits
rules.requirement: Comprehensive individualized treatment plan finalized within the first three outpatient visits.

rules.label: Treatment plan review every 90 days
rules.requirement: Treatment plan formally reviewed and updated at least every 90 days.

rules.label: Treatment plan full rewrite annually
rules.requirement: Treatment plan revised and rewritten in its entirety at least every 365 days.

rules.label: Treatment plan sign-off credential gate
rules.requirement: Treatment plans developed, approved, and signed by an LMHP or QAP with appropriate routing.

rules.label: Diagnoses restricted to DSM-5-TR
rules.requirement: All diagnoses rendered per DSM-5-TR using active codes/specifiers.

rules.label: Diagnostic privilege gate
rules.requirement: Diagnoses restricted to LMHPs. Non-licensed (QAP/QMHP) intake routes to LMHP via dual-signature workflow. Unsupervised LMSW blocked.

entry_rule.label: OMU screening precedes level placement
entry_rule.requirement: All in-state SATOP clients enter via OMU standardized screening, conducted by a credentialed SQP, before level assignment.

additional_constraint.label: At least 10 hours focused explicitly on drinking-and-driving issues

rules.label: CIP delivered by QSAP
rules.requirement: Intensive outpatient (CIP) services delivered by a QSAP; associates only under supervision.

SROP.label: Level IV — Serious and Repeat Offender Program

rules.label: SROP minimum 90 calendar days
rules.requirement: Program delivered over no less than 90 calendar days.

comparable_out_of_state.label: Comparable Out-of-State Program (in lieu of SATOP)

rules.label: Comparable program 120 total hours
rules.note: Remaining hours: driver-related education, group education, or family therapy.

rules.label: Comparable program within 6 months of assessment
Out-of-state provider must be certified/accredited
rules.requirement: Provider state-certified or accredited by recognized national body (e.g., The Joint Commission, CARF).

rules.label: Comparable Program Completion Form sections + supplemental fee
rules.requirement: Track/store Sections II, III, IV of the Comparable Program Completion Form; verify $249 supplemental fee submitted to MO DMH.

rules.label: Direct-care staff ASAM-trained
rules.requirement: All direct care staff trained in ASAM Criteria (2013, 3rd ed.), co-occurring disorders, Zero Suicide prevention, trauma-informed care.

rules.label: QAP/QMHP intake dual-signature routing
rules.requirement: When QAP/QMHP conducts intake, diagnostic and clinical summary routed to and co-signed by an approved LMHP with dated e-signature.

rules.label: Group size avg ≤ 12 per facilitator per month
rules.requirement: Group counseling capped at a strict average of 12 individuals per facilitator per calendar month.

rules.label: Group log with exact in/out times
rules.requirement: Secure group log capturing service type, summary, date, and exact attendee check-in/check-out times.

rules.label: Per-session facilitator signature (no stamps)
rules.requirement: Block pre-saved signature stamps; facilitator must authenticate signature for each session.

REACT.label: Required Educational Assessment and Community Treatment

rules.label: Out-of-state relocation reporting
rules.requirement: If offender relocates, equivalent accredited program completed and compliance reported to supervising MO Probation & Parole officer.

rules.label: Single comprehensive TPO consent allowed
rules.requirement: Patient may sign a single written consent authorizing all future TPO uses/disclosures.

rules.label: Hard stop: cannot combine TPO + legal-proceeding consent
rules.requirement: Consent for use/disclosure in civil/criminal/administrative/legislative proceedings must be a separate standalone form; combining with TPO is prohibited.

rules.label: Mandatory § 2.32 redisclosure notice
rules.requirement: Every consented disclosure must append the § 2.32 notice prohibiting further redisclosure unless permitted.

rules.label: Immutable consent + disclosure audit log
rules.requirement: Maintain audit logs of all active/expired/revoked consents and all partner-portal views, downloads, and transfers.
rules.note: ENFORCEMENT REQUIRES the trust-layer rebuild (audit_logs). Encoding this rule does NOT make the app 42-CFR compliant on its own.

rules.label: Priority population flagging
rules.requirement: Flag and prioritize: pregnant women who inject drugs; pregnant women; persons who injected in past 30 days; civil involuntary commitments; high-risk P&P/DOC referrals; TANF referrals; Children's System of Care.

rag_handoff._comment: These items are EXPLANATORY and belong in the Clara knowledge base (pgvector), NOT in this deterministic pack. Listed here only as a manifest of what to route to RAG.
topics: What SATOP is and why completion is required for license reinstatement
Plain-language explanation of each SATOP level and who it's for
What a comparable out-of-state program is and how the process works
Plain-language 42 CFR Part 2 explanation (redisclosure, bifurcated consent, why it matters for court-involved clients)
Credential definitions in plain language (what an LMHP/QAP/SQP/QSAP is)
Fee structures and funding (screening fee, supplemental fee, Mental Health Earnings Fund)
Recovery support, care coordination, CCBHC alignment context
